tool_design.role_in_intervention: Venue operating standard defining exactly how B001 is executed and taught.
tool_design.usage_moment: At rollout, onboarding, and any major process change
tool_design.expected_output: Issued SOP with completed unit register and staff sign-off

failure_warnings.warning: SOP is issued but not briefed or practiced.
failure_warnings.what_can_go_wrong: The standard exists on paper only.
People bypass it because it feels outdated.
early_signals: Users say it no longer fits

content:
# Temperature Monitoring SOP & Team Sign-Off **Tool ID:** T001-05 **Block:** B001 (Daily Temperature Monitoring) **Domain:** Food Safety & Hygiene (D01_food_safety) **Level:** L2 **Format:** SOP **Role:** Manager / Owner **Time Required:** 20 minutes to issue, 5 minutes to brief each team member **Version:** 2.0 --- ## What This Tool Is For Use this SOP to define exactly how your venue performs temperature monitoring, who owns it, what "good" looks like, and how records are kept inspection-ready. This is the operating standard behind `T001-01`, `T001-02`, `T001-03`, and `T001-04`. --- ## Scope This SOP applies to: - all chilled and frozen storage units used for food or milk - all staff who record or review temperatures - all temperature monitoring records retained for compliance --- ## Roles And Responsibility | Role | Responsibility | |---|---| | Opening lead | Complete the first temperature round before service starts | | Afternoon lead | Complete the second round during the agreed check window | | Manager | Review logs daily, close corrective actions, maintain calibration record | | Owner | Review monthly dashboard, resource repairs or replacement | --- ## Venue Unit Register Complete this before issuing the SOP. | Unit Name | Unit Type | Safe Range | Probe Point / Product | Check Windows | Notes | |---|---|---|---|---|---| | Milk fridge | Chilled | `1-5C` | | Opening / Afternoon | | | Prep fridge | Chilled | `1-5C` | | Opening / Afternoon | | | Display fridge | Chilled | `1-5C` | | Opening / Afternoon | | | Walk-in / back-up fridge | Chilled | `1-5C` | | Opening / Afternoon | | | Main freezer | Frozen | `-18C` or below | | Opening / Afternoon | | | Other | | | | | | --- ## Standard Checking Method
  • Wash hands and sanitise the probe before use.
  • Follow the unit register in the same order each round.
  • Probe the agreed product or proxy point for each unit.
  • Wait for a stable reading before recording.
  • Record the real number immediately on [T001-01].
  • Sanitise the probe between units.
--- ## Safe Limits And Triggers | Scenario | Required Response | |---|---| | Fridge within `1-5C` | Record and continue | | Fridge above `5C` but below `8C` | Investigate simple cause, recheck, note action | | Fridge `8C` or above | Alert manager and open [T001-03] | | Freezer above `-15C` | Investigate immediately and note action | | Freezer `-12C` or warmer | Alert manager and open [T001-03] | | Probe not calibrated | Stop temperature round until valid device is available | --- ## Record-Keeping Rules - `T001-01` is completed twice daily and filed the same day. - `T001-02` is updated monthly and whenever the probe is questioned or dropped. - `T001-03` is opened for any true trigger event or repeat warning pattern. - `T001-04` is completed monthly by management. - Keep all records for at least 12 months in the food safety file or digital equivalent. --- ## Daily Manager Review Manager must confirm: - both rounds are present - any high readings include action notes - any trigger event has the matching record - any maintenance risk is captured for follow-up - logs are signed before filing --- ## When This SOP Must Be Reviewed Review immediately if: - a new cold unit is added or removed - the check windows change - the probe type changes - an EHO or internal audit identifies a weakness - repeat temperature drift appears in the same unit Otherwise review at least once every 12 months. --- ## Team Briefing And Sign-Off Use this table to confirm every relevant team member has been briefed. | Team Member | Role | Briefed On | Signature / Initials | Trainer | Date | |---|---|---|---|---|---| | | | `T001-01` / `T001-03` | | | | | | | `T001-01` / `T001-03` | | | | | | | `T001-01` / `T001-03` | | | | | | | `T001-01` / `T001-03` | | | | | | | `T001-01` / `T001-03` | | | | --- ## Connected Resources **Core tools in this block:** - T001-01 - Daily Temperature Log & Check Sheet - T001-02 - Probe Calibration Record & Accuracy Tracker - T001-03 - Temperature Excursion Trigger Log - T001-04 - Monthly Temperature Compliance Dashboard **Related blocks:** - B002 - Cold Chain Management SOP - B003 - Temperature Excursion Response --- *Tool Version 2.0 - the house standard behind the daily log.*
